title: Add login authentication, role-based access control, and admin user management

requirements.summary: Replace Internet Identity with username/password login screen and store authenticated user in React context
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users see only the login screen
Incorrect credentials show an error message
Successful login navigates to the dashboard
Logged-in user info (name, role) is accessible throughout the app

file_operations.description: Create an AuthContext and AuthProvider to manage the currently authenticated user state (id, username, role) throughout the app. Expose login, logout, and user state via context. The login function calls the backend login() method with username and password, stores the returned user info on success, and provides error handling for invalid credentials.

Create a LoginScreen component displaying the app logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/verifone-logo.dim_256x256.png), username field, password field, and Sign In button. On submit, call the login function from AuthContext. Show error messages for authentication failures. On success, navigate to the dashboard.

Wrap the app with AuthProvider from AuthContext. Replace the Internet Identity authentication gate with a check for the authenticated user from AuthContext. If no user is authenticated, render LoginScreen instead of the router. Remove references to InternetIdentityProvider and the profile setup modal.
Remove the InternetIdentityProvider wrapper since Internet Identity is no longer used for authentication.

requirements.summary: Add admin-only Settings page and conditionally render Settings link in Sidebar
acceptanceCriteria: Settings link appears in the sidebar only for admin users
Regular users cannot access /settings directly (redirected away)
Admin users can navigate to the Settings page

file_operations.description: Update the SettingsPage component to add an admin-only route guard at the top of the component. Use the authenticated user from AuthContext to check if the user has the Admin role (ManagedUserRole.Admin). If not admin, redirect to the dashboard and display an access denied message. Retain the existing user management and client management sections.

Update the Sidebar component to conditionally render the Settings navigation link only when the authenticated user from AuthContext has the Admin role (ManagedUserRole.Admin). Remove the Internet Identity-based isAdmin check and replace with a check against the AuthContext user role.
Add route registration for /settings that renders SettingsPage. Ensure the route is protected by checking the authenticated user role in AuthContext.

requirements.summary: Build User Management section in Settings page with create, edit, delete user dialogs
acceptanceCriteria: User table lists all users with username and role columns
Create user dialog validates that username and password are non-empty
Edit user dialog pre-fills existing username and role
Delete prompts for confirmation before removing
Logged-in admin cannot delete their own account

file_operations.description: Create a dialog component for adding a new user with fields for username, password, and role (Admin or User). Validate that username and password are non-empty before submission. Call the createUser mutation from useQueries. Show success/error toasts and close dialog on success.

Create a dialog component for editing an existing user. Pre-fill the username and role fields from the selected user. Allow updating username, password (optional if blank), and role. Call the updateUser mutation from useQueries. Show success/error toasts and close dialog on success.

Add a User Management section displaying a table of all users (fetched via getUsers query) with columns for username and role. Include action buttons: Create New User (opens CreateUserDialog), Edit (opens EditUserDialog with selected user), and Delete (shows confirmation prompt). Prevent deletion of the currently logged-in admin user by comparing the user id from AuthContext with the table row user id. Use the existing query hooks from useQueries for fetching, creating, updating, and deleting users.

Verify that query hooks for getUsers, createUser, updateUser, and deleteUser are present and correctly configured. If missing, add them using the backend interface methods. Ensure mutations invalidate the 'users' query key on success.

requirements.summary: Enforce role-based access control using the locally stored user role from AuthContext
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only UI elements are hidden for regular users
Role gating works based on the username/password login session role
Regular users can view data but cannot create, edit, or delete records

file_operations.description: Replace the Internet Identity-based isAdmin check with a role check using the authenticated user from AuthContext. Show add/edit/delete/import buttons only when the user has the Admin role (ManagedUserRole.Admin).

Replace the Internet Identity-based isAdmin check with a role check using the authenticated user from AuthContext. Show add part and adjust stock buttons only when the user has the Admin role (ManagedUserRole.Admin).

Replace the Internet Identity-based isAdmin check with a role check using the authenticated user from AuthContext. Show create new ticket and edit buttons only when the user has the Admin role (ManagedUserRole.Admin).

Replace the Internet Identity-based isAdmin check with a role check using the authenticated user from AuthContext. Enable editing of diagnosis, outcome, and parts replaced fields only when the user has the Admin role (ManagedUserRole.Admin).
If the modal contains any admin-only actions, replace the Internet Identity-based isAdmin check with a role check using the authenticated user from AuthContext.

requirements.summary: Update Header to display logged-in username and role, and replace logout with session clearing
acceptanceCriteria: Header shows the current user's name and role badge
Logout button clears session and shows the login screen
No Internet Identity references remain visible in the UI

file_operations.description: Update the Header component to display the authenticated user's username and role badge from AuthContext instead of the Internet Identity user info. Replace the Internet Identity logout button with a logout button that calls the logout function from AuthContext, clears the local session, and returns to the login screen. Remove all references to Internet Identity.
